calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Bottom Line Food Processors, Inc.S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents [Set One] And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT BOTTOM LINE FOOD PROCESSORS, INC.'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS [SET ONE] AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: motion to compel further responses to set one document requests from defendant Bottom Line Food Processors and request for monetary sanctions is denied as moot as to the motion to compel and granted in part as to the request for monetary sanctions. No later than April 10, 2026 Bottom line and its counsel must pay $2,080 in reasonable monetary sanctions to Environmental Health. Bottom Line's recent service of supplemental responses moots the motion to compel. Bottom Line's long delay in revising the protective order causing Environmental Health to reasonably file this motion lacked substantial justification. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Plaintiff Environmental Health Advocates, Inc.S Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Bottom Line Food Processors, Inc.S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories [Set One] And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT BOTTOM LINE FOOD PROCESSORS, INC.'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES [SET ONE] AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Environmental Health Advocates, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to set one special interrogatories from defendant Bottom Line Food Processors and request for monetary sanctions is denied as moot as to the motion to compel and granted in part as to the request for monetary sanctions. No later than April 10, 2026 Bottom line and its counsel must pay $1,080 in reasonable monetary sanctions to Environmental Health. Bottom Line's recent service of supplemental responses moots the motion to compel. Bottom Line's long delay in revising the protective order causing Environmental Health to reasonably file this motion lacked substantial justification. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Set For Trial On Dec-07-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
